questions.prompt: For an Oregon contractor with paid employees working in Oregon, which step is required before issuing paychecks?
choices: Register for a Business Identification Number (BIN)
Apply for a TriMet district tax exemption
Submit Form WR for the prior year
questions.explanation: Oregon DOR states employers with paid employees working in Oregon should register for a BIN before issuing paychecks; the other options are different tax processes.

questions.prompt: Which statement best describes Oregon's Statewide Transit Tax (STT) for exam purposes?
choices: It is employee-wage withholding administered by DOR under ORS 320.550
It is an employer-side district payroll tax only
It applies to self-employment income but not wages
It replaces Oregon withholding and combined payroll reporting
questions.explanation: DOR describes STT as statewide wage withholding tied to ORS 320.550 and wage definitions, not a replacement for other payroll reporting and not self-employment withholding.

questions.prompt: Under Oregon DOR CAT guidance, when does filing/payment generally begin?
choices: When taxable Oregon commercial activity exceeds $1 million
As soon as any Oregon revenue is earned
Only after taxable Oregon commercial activity exceeds $5 million
Only when the business has Oregon payroll employees
questions.explanation: DOR's threshold framework distinguishes registration from filing/payment, with filing/payment generally triggered above $1 million of taxable Oregon commercial activity.
